questions.text: What miracle does the first Ayah of this Surah describe?
questions.answer: The Night Journey (Al-Isra) of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) from Al-Masjid al-Haram in Makkah to Al-Masjid al-Aqsa in Jerusalem.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the Night Journey as stated in the opening of the Surah?
questions.answer: To show the Prophet (SAW) some of Allah's signs.

questions.text: Which Prophet was given the Scripture as a guidance for the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: Prophet Musa (AS).

questions.text: What did Allah decree for the Children of Israel regarding their behavior on Earth?
questions.answer: That they would cause corruption twice and reach a level of great haughtiness.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Quran's guidance for mankind?
questions.answer: It guides to that which is most upright and gives glad tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds.

questions.text: What is the nature of man according to this Surah when he prays?
questions.answer: Man supplicates for evil just as he supplicates for good, for man is ever hasty.

questions.text: What are the two signs mentioned that Allah has made as a means to know the count of years?
questions.answer: The night and the day; Allah made the sign of the night dim and the sign of the day bright.

questions.text: What is attached to the neck of every human being?
questions.answer: Their 'bird' (record of deeds), which will be shown as an open book on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What will be told to every person as they receive their record of deeds?
questions.answer: 'Read your record. Sufficient is your soul today as an accountant against you.'

questions.text: Does one person bear the burden of another person's sins?
questions.answer: No, 'no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another.'

questions.text: Does Allah punish a people before sending a messenger to them?
questions.answer: No, 'We would never punish until We sent a messenger.'

questions.text: What is the difference between those who desire the fleeting life (this world) and those who desire the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Those who desire this world are given what Allah wills, but their end is Hell; those who desire the Hereafter and strive for it with faith will have their effort appreciated.

questions.text: What is the command regarding treatment of parents in this Surah?
questions.answer: To worship only Allah and to show kindness to parents, especially in their old age, never saying even 'Uff' (a sigh of frustration) to them.

questions.text: What prayer are believers taught for their parents?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up [when I was] small.'

questions.text: Apart from parents, who else are we commanded to give their due rights?
questions.answer: The relatives, the needy, and the traveler.

questions.text: How does the Surah characterize those who spend wastefully (the Tabdhir)?
questions.answer: They are brothers of the devils (Ikhwan al-Shayatin), and Shaitan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding moderation in spending?
questions.answer: Do not keep your hand chained to your neck (be stingy) nor extend it to its full stretch (be wasteful), lest you become blamed and insolvent.

questions.text: What is the Quranic stance on taking the life of a child due to fear of poverty?
questions.answer: It is strictly forbidden; Allah provides for them and for the parents. Killing them is a great sin.

questions.text: What is the command regarding Zina (unlawful sexual intercourse)?
questions.answer: Do not even approach it; indeed, it is an immorality and an evil way.

questions.text: What is the rule regarding killing a soul?
questions.answer: Do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden, except by right (legal justice).

questions.text: What is the command regarding the property of an orphan?
questions.answer: Do not approach it except in the best way (to improve it) until he reaches maturity.

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding fulfilling promises and covenants?
questions.answer: Fulfill the covenant, for the covenant will be questioned on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the command regarding weights and measures in trade?
questions.answer: Give full measure when you measure and weigh with an even balance.

questions.text: What is the warning against following something you have no knowledge of?
questions.answer: Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge; indeed, the hearing, the sight, and the heart—all those will be questioned.

questions.text: What is the command regarding walking on the earth with pride?
questions.answer: Do not walk with insolence, for you cannot tear the earth apart nor reach the mountains in height.

questions.text: How do the seven heavens and the earth and all in them regard Allah?
questions.answer: They exalt Him, but mankind does not understand their way of exalting (Tasbih).

questions.text: What is placed between the Prophet and those who do not believe in the Hereafter when he recites the Quran?
questions.answer: A concealed veil, so they do not understand the message.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when they are raised from the dead?
questions.answer: They will think they remained in their graves for only a very short time.

questions.text: How should believers speak to others, especially in disputes?
questions.answer: They should say that which is best, for Shaitan seeks to sow discord between them.

questions.text: Who are the beings called upon besides Allah that themselves seek a way to their Lord?
questions.answer: Even the righteous beings (like angels or prophets) whom people worship seek nearness to Allah and fear His punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Cursed Tree' mentioned in the Quran?
questions.answer: It refers to the Tree of Zaqqum in Hell, which is a trial for people.

questions.text: Why did Iblis refuse to prostrate to Adam according to this Surah?
questions.answer: He said, 'Should I prostrate to one You created from clay?'

questions.text: What did Iblis vow to do to the descendants of Adam?
questions.answer: To bring them under his control, except for a few.

questions.text: What are the tools Shaitan uses to mislead people?
questions.answer: His voice, his cavalry and infantry (allies), and sharing in their wealth and children, making them false promises.

questions.text: Who are the only ones Shaitan has no authority over?
questions.answer: The true servants of Allah; for them, Allah is sufficient as a Guardian.

questions.text: What is the favor Allah has bestowed upon the Children of Adam?
questions.answer: He has honored them, carried them on land and sea, provided them with good things, and preferred them over much of His creation.

questions.text: How will every people be called on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will be called with their 'Imam' (their leader or their record of deeds).

questions.text: What happens to the one who is 'blind' (spiritually) in this life?
questions.answer: He will be blind in the Hereafter and even more astray from the path.

questions.text: What was the attempt of the disbelievers regarding the Prophet's revelation?
questions.answer: They tried to tempt him away from what was revealed to him so he might invent something else about Allah.

questions.text: What are the times of prayer mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: From the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night, and the dawn prayer (Quran al-Fajr).

questions.text: What is the special status of the dawn prayer?
questions.answer: It is witnessed by the angels.

questions.text: What is the 'Tahajjud' prayer and what is its reward for the Prophet?
questions.answer: A prayer in the night as an additional duty; through it, Allah would raise him to a 'Praised Station' (Maqam-um-Mahmuda).

questions.text: What prayer did the Prophet use for a truthful beginning and end?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, cause me to enter a truthful entrance and cause me to exit a truthful exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority.'

questions.text: What happens when the truth arrives regarding falsehood?
questions.answer: 'Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed, falsehood is bound to depart.'

questions.text: How does Allah describe the Quran's effect on believers vs. wrongdoers?
questions.answer: It is a healing and mercy for believers, but increases the wrongdoers only in loss.

questions.text: How does man react when Allah bestows favor upon him compared to when evil touches him?
questions.answer: When favored, he turns away and distances himself; when evil touches him, he is in despair.

questions.text: What is the 'Ruh' (Soul/Spirit) according to this Surah?
questions.answer: It is of the affair/command of my Lord, and mankind has been given but little knowledge of it.

questions.text: What was the challenge regarding the Quran mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: If mankind and jinn joined together to produce the like of the Quran, they could not, even if they assisted each other.

questions.text: Name three of the physical miracles the disbelievers demanded from the Prophet.
questions.answer: Causing a spring to gush from the earth, having a garden of palms and grapes with rivers, or bringing Allah and the angels before them.

questions.text: What was the disbelievers' objection to a human messenger?
questions.answer: They said, 'Has Allah sent a human as a messenger?'

questions.text: Why did Allah send a human Messenger instead of an Angel?
questions.answer: Because if there were angels walking on earth in peace, Allah would have sent down an angel as a messenger to them.

questions.text: How will the disbelievers be gathered on the Day of Resurrection?
questions.answer: On their faces—blind, dumb, and deaf; their refuge is Hell.

questions.text: How many clear signs were given to Prophet Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: Nine clear signs.

questions.text: What did Fir'aun say to Musa when he came with the signs?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, I think you, O Musa, are affected by magic.'

questions.text: What was Musa's response to Fir'aun's accusation of magic?
questions.answer: He said, 'You have already known that none has sent down these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as evidence, and indeed, I think you, O Pharaoh, are destroyed.'

questions.text: What happened to Fir'aun and those with him when he tried to drive the Children of Israel from the land?
questions.answer: Allah drowned them all together.

questions.text: What was promised to the Children of Israel after Fir'aun's drowning?
questions.answer: To dwell in the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter comes, Allah will bring them all together.

questions.text: What is the reaction of those who were given knowledge before when the Quran is recited to them?
questions.answer: They fall upon their faces in prostration, weeping, and it increases them in humility.

questions.text: What names should be used to call upon Allah?
questions.answer: Call Him Allah or Ar-Rahman; to Him belong the best names (Al-Asma'ul Husna).

questions.text: What is the instruction regarding the tone of voice during prayer?
questions.answer: Do not be too loud in your prayer nor too quiet, but seek a way in between.

questions.text: What is the final praise mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Praise to Allah, who has not taken a son and has had no partner in [His] dominion and has no need of a protector out of weakness; and magnify Him with [great] magnificence.

questions.text: What is the 'Night Journey' (Isra)?
questions.answer: The miraculous horizontal travel of the Prophet from Makkah to Jerusalem.

questions.text: What is the 'Sacred Mosque' (Al-Masjid al-Haram)?
questions.answer: The mosque in Makkah surrounding the Kaaba.

questions.text: What is the 'Farthest Mosque' (Al-Masjid al-Aqsa)?
questions.answer: The mosque in Jerusalem whose surroundings Allah has blessed.

questions.text: What is the 'corruption twice' mentioned for the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: Two historical periods of widespread disobedience and arrogance that led to their downfall.

questions.text: What is the 'haughtiness' (Uluww) of the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: Their excessive pride and rebellion against Allah's laws.

questions.text: What is the 'sign of the night'?
questions.answer: The darkness and the moon, made less bright for rest and time-keeping.

questions.text: What is the 'sign of the day'?
questions.answer: The sunlight, made bright for people to seek Allah's bounty.

questions.text: What is the 'Accountant' soul?
questions.answer: The person's own awareness and the record of their deeds on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'Mercy' for parents?
questions.answer: The kindness and care children should provide them in their old age.

questions.text: What is 'wastefulness' (Tabdhir)?
questions.answer: Spending wealth on sinful things or in an excessive, pointless manner.

questions.text: What is the 'Covenant' (Ahd)?
questions.answer: The promises made to Allah and between people.

questions.text: What is 'insolence' in walking?
questions.answer: An arrogant or boastful manner of movement.

questions.text: What is the 'Tasbih' of the universe?
questions.answer: The continuous praise and glorification of Allah by all created things.

questions.text: What is the 'veil' (Hijab)?
questions.answer: A spiritual barrier that prevents the arrogant from understanding the Quran.

questions.text: What is the 'supporting authority' (Sultanun Nasira)?
questions.answer: The divine support and power given by Allah to the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'healing' (Shifa) of the Quran?
questions.answer: Its ability to guide the soul and cure the diseases of the heart.

questions.text: What is the 'Spirit' (Ruh)?
questions.answer: The life-force or the angel Jibril, whose nature is known only to Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Praised Station' (Maqam-um-Mahmuda)?
questions.answer: A high rank of honor and intercession for the Prophet in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Final Prayer' of the Surah?
questions.answer: The declaration of Allah's oneness, self-sufficiency, and greatness.
